rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use visible identifiers from the current scope, not global names
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component structure from the input AADL model
Preserve the original AADL architecture during fusion
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions without explicit model exposure
Do not use event ports in numeric or Boolean comparisons without explicit data values
